product_brief: Hardware security platform for 5G & Telecom Security focusing on quantum-safe cryptography.
pqc_capability_description: G+D is developing prototypes in Digital Security, Financial Platforms, and Currency Technology segments. The company states it is positioned to deliver products enhanced with PQC capabilities as standards become finalized. No specific algorithms or production-ready status for the SIM platform itself are stated; only general involvement in standardization and prototype development is mentioned.
crypto_primitives: RSA, ECC (Elliptic Curve Cryptography), AES (Advanced Encryption Standard)
architecture_type: Hardware (SIM/eSIM/iSIM context implied by product name and category)
target_industries: Telecommunications, Mobile Network Operators, IoT Service Provider, Government & Public Sector, Banking & Payments
pqc_roadmap_details: Developing prototypes; positioned to deliver PQC-enhanced products as NIST standards (FIPS 203, FIPS 204, FIPS 205) are finalized. Mentions a backup key encapsulation mechanism standard will be developed in coming years.
signature_schemes: RSA, ECC (Current); PQC algorithms (Planned/Standardized: Module-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Standard, Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Standard)

product_name: Samsung Knox Quantum-Safe (specifically Post-Quantum Enhanced Data Protection within Samsung Knox Matrix)
product_brief: A mobile security feature integrating post-quantum cryptography into Samsung Knox Matrix for cloud data protection.
pqc_support: Yes (with details: Integrated into Samsung Knox Matrix for Galaxy S25 series, using NIST standards like ML-KEM).
pqc_capability_description: Samsung has integrated Post-Quantum Enhanced Data Protection (EDP) into Knox Matrix to secure cloud backups and synced data. It utilizes the ML-KEM (Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism) algorithm, following NIST standards. The feature is available on the Galaxy S25 series running One UI 7, providing end-to-end encryption against quantum threats for smartphones, TVs, and digital appliances.
fips_validated: No (Document mentions NIST finalized standards FIPS 203, 204, 205, but does not state the product itself is FIPS validated).
primary_platforms: Galaxy S25 series, One UI 7, Samsung Cloud
target_industries: Mobile, Consumer Electronics, Enterprise (implied by Knox Matrix context)
pqc_roadmap_details: Currently available on Galaxy S25 series; Samsung plans to continue pioneering mobile security as quantum computing evolves.
signature_schemes: Not stated (Document mentions ML-KEM for key encapsulation and references NIST FIPS 204/205 standards but does not explicitly state which signature scheme is implemented in this specific product).
authoritative_source_url: https://news.samsung.com/ (inferred from "Samsung Newsroom" context, though exact article URL not provided in text)

product_name: IDEMIA PQC Secure Element (Hardware Accelerator based on Keccak)
product_brief: Advanced post-quantum hardware accelerator based on Keccak, embedded in future quantum-safe chips.
pqc_support: Yes (with details: Hardware accelerator for NIST standardized algorithms including ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA)
pqc_capability_description: An advanced hardware accelerator based on the Keccak function designed to offload intensive post-quantum tasks from the main processor. It supports quantum-resistant algorithms standardized by NIST, specifically ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204), and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205). The module is embedded in all future quantum-safe chips from IDEMIA Secure Transactions to ensure high performance and seamless transition.
key_management_model: Hardware-based key storage and generation within secure elements/chips; specific injection methods not stated.
architecture_type: Hardware accelerator embedded in secure chips (Secure Element)
fips_validated: No (References FIPS 203, 204, 205 standards but does not state the product itself is FIPS validated)
primary_platforms: Embedded in future quantum-safe chips from IDEMIA Secure Transactions; applicable to banking, automotive, IoT, Industry 4.0, healthcare, payment cards, crypto wallets, SIM cards, NFC products.
target_industries: Banking, Automotive, IoT, Industry 4.0, Healthcare, Payment, Connectivity
pqc_roadmap_details: The accelerator will be embedded in all future quantum-safe chips from IDEMIA Secure Transactions to support migration to NIST standardized algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA).
